message:
fix(codegen): Fix comparison dunder methods and pickle attribute resolution

Fixes:
1. Comparison operator dunder methods (.Lt, .LtEq, .Gt, .GtEq, .Eq, .NotEq)
   now properly wrap with runtime.toBool(try ...) for both left-side and
   right-side (reflected) class instance cases. Dunder methods return
   error unions with object types that need toBool() conversion.

2. pickle module constants (HIGHEST_PROTOCOL, DEFAULT_PROTOCOL) and
   other attribute-style accesses now emit directly to output buffer
   instead of using ZigBuilder. The builder writes to separate buffers
   not visible to the output length check in genAttribute.

3. Added 'pickle' to the confidence whitelist in type inference so
   pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL is treated as certain, avoiding unnecessary
   PyValue wrapping in range() loops.

files.patch:
@@ -497,6 +497,7 @@ pub fn inferExprTyped(
                     std.mem.eql(u8, obj_name, "hashlib") or
                     std.mem.eql(u8, obj_name, "time") or
                     std.mem.eql(u8, obj_name, "platform") or
+                    std.mem.eql(u8, obj_name, "pickle") or // pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL, etc.
                     std.mem.eql(u8, obj_name, "re"))
                 {
                     break :blk .certain;
